about.label: What is Agarwood?
about.headline: A Tree's Wound Becomes the World's Rarest Gift
about.body: Agarwood forms when an Aquilaria tree secretes resin to protect itself from injury. Over decades, this resin hardens into dark, fragrant heartwood — more precious than gold. Used in traditional medicine for over 3,000 years, it remains one of the most sought-after natural substances on Earth.

values.label: Our Core Values
values.headline: Nature, Quality & Trust — the Three Pillars
values.body: Every step of our process — from forest to final product — is guided by an unwavering respect for nature, rigorous quality standards, and full transparency toward our customers and partners.

sourcing.description: The northern highlands of Vietnam — premium-grade Aquilaria crassna with exceptional resin density. Our Vietnamese partners hold 15+ years of cultivation expertise.
Sumatra and Kalimantan rainforests — naturally-formed Aquilaria malaccensis, considered among the finest aromatic profiles in the world.
Cambodian highland forests — renowned for distinctive deep, complex fragrance profiles preferred in Middle Eastern and East Asian markets.

faq.question: Do you ship internationally?
faq.answer: Yes — we ship worldwide. Shipping costs and delivery times vary by destination. Contact us for a detailed quote based on your location and order size.

faq.question: What is your minimum wholesale order?
faq.answer: Wholesale and B2B orders typically start at 10kg of raw material or 100 units of finished product. Contact our partnerships team to discuss your specific requirements.

faq.question: Can I verify product authenticity?
faq.answer: Every product comes with a certificate of authenticity, species verification documentation, and a unique tracking number. We also provide HACCP and ISO 22000 documentation upon request.

faq.question: When will capsules and ampoules be available?
faq.answer: Both products are currently in final development. Subscribe to our newsletter or submit a contact form with category 'Product Information' to be notified at launch.

blogFeatured.title: Agarwood's 3,000-Year History: From Ancient Egypt to Modern Luxury by mj
blogFeatured.excerpt: A comprehensive timeline tracing how the world's most prized aromatic wood travelled from Southeast Asian forests through ancient trade routes, royal courts, Buddhist temples, and finally to the global luxury market.
